description: Rustynet MCP server manifest — three stdio JSON-RPC servers providing repo context, gate running, and lab state management for AI agents.

servers.name: rustynet-mcp-repo-context
servers.description: Structured access to the Rustynet documentation graph, requirements, security controls/findings, architecture constraints, role transitions, platform support, and crate structure. which_crate gives a file's layer + boundary rule; get_crate_dependencies gives a crate's forward + reverse deps (blast radius before patching a shared crate). 17 tools + MCP resources (docs) + prompts.
servers.resources: Curated documentation index exposed as rustynet-doc:// resources (resources/list + resources/read).
servers.working_dir: <baked at build time via build.rs; CWD-independent>

servers.name: rustynet-mcp-gate-runner
servers.description: Runs Rustynet quality gates on demand — cargo fmt, check, clippy, test, build, audit, deny, and CI gate scripts. run_security_gates bundles the local security-critical suite (secrets hygiene, backend-boundary leakage, dataplane-leak, security-regression, supply-chain, role-auth, secret-redaction, traversal-adversarial, active-network) + audit/deny into one pass/fail verdict — the 'did I break security?' check. list_gate_scripts is categorized (security / role / phase / release / lab); run_gate_scripts runs any chosen set. Every command runs under a kill-on-timeout watchdog. 11 tools.
servers.working_dir: <baked at build time via build.rs; CWD-independent>

servers.name: rustynet-mcp-lab-state
servers.description: Queries/manages the UTM VM lab AND drives the autonomous overnight live-lab loop (run → catch bugs → patch → re-verify) via async background jobs. Live-lab runs launch detached (start_live_lab_run returns a job_id instantly, survives an MCP-server reload); poll get_job_status (completion-record-first, pid-reuse-safe), read structured results with get_run_result, read logs with bounded tail_job_log / read_report_artifact, search them with grep_report, jump to a stage's evidence with get_stage_log, reclaim disk with prune_jobs. Track convergence across iterations with get_run_trend (GREEN / STUCK / MOVING verdict). Runs default to source-mode working-tree (deploys uncommitted patches), auto-topology (fills Windows+macOS from the inventory for full 3-OS coverage), and accept a >24h timeout_secs. Fast re-verify after a per-node code patch: rebuild_nodes redeploys only the affected node (others keep state) + skip_soak — no full multi-node rebuild. (No mid-test-stage resume exists; setup-stage failures can resume_from=prepare_source_archive. explain_stage spells out the exact re-verify command.) wait_for_job blocks until done (no busy-poll); explain_stage maps a failed stage to its owning file + common causes; get_lab_topology gives a secret-free node/role digest. Full VM power control (power_on_vm / power_off_vm[force] / get_vm_power_state), reachability triage (check_vm_reachable = power-state + TCP probe), out-of-band Linux-guest diagnostics + network reset for unreachable guests (get_vm_network_info / reset_vm_network via utmctl exec, no SSH), and host_disk_status let an agent recover the lab and manage disk over a long run with no human. preflight_check is a one-call loop-start go/no-go (host tools + ssh material + inventory + disk + deploy set + every node's power/TCP). what_will_deploy previews exactly what a run ships to the VMs for a source_mode (tracked in, untracked out) so a patch is never tested stale. diff_runs compares two runs' stage outcomes (helped vs regressed). write_loop_note / get_loop_journal are a durable append-only journal under state/ that survives context compaction, so the agent remembers what it already tried across a 24h+ run. get_run_progress gives live mid-run visibility (elapsed, last-activity age with a hang flag, latest log lines, artifacts so far) between wait_for_job calls. find_untested_work aggregates the run-matrix history into a prioritized work queue (regressed / never-passed / never-run / stale-green) so the agent is handed a target instead of hunting. seed_cargo_cache re-syncs each guest's OFFLINE cargo registry to the workspace Cargo.lock after a lock change (tar+scp only the missing .crate blobs + sparse-index entries into the guest registry root, Unix + Windows/PowerShell) so `cargo build --offline` stops failing with `no matching package`. Short ops run under a kill-on-timeout watchdog. 43 tools + an overnight-loop prompt.
servers.working_dir: <baked at build time via build.rs; CWD-independent>

notes: The repo root is baked into each binary at compile time (build.rs), so the servers resolve documents/ and crates/ regardless of the client's working directory. Override with the RUSTYNET_REPO_ROOT env var if needed. rustynet-mcp-deepseek's deepseek_agent reads the LOCAL repo (read-only) and resolves the baked repo root like the other servers; its three proxy tools (read/write/read_write) do not need it.

deepseek_server.name: rustynet-mcp-deepseek
deepseek_server.description: Calls the DeepSeek API as a first-class sub-agent. Four tools: read (analysis/research), write (generation), read_write (analyze then generate) — these three are pure prompt+context proxies — plus deepseek_agent, a READ-ONLY autonomous research agent that drives an OpenAI-style tool-calling loop over a confined read-only tool set of 20 tools (read_file, list_dir, grep, find_files, find_definition, read-only git, utm_vm_status, lab_inventory, lab_node_reachable, lab_guest_exec [one of four FIXED read-only Linux-guest commands — network/routes/dns/daemon — via utmctl exec; NOT caller-controlled], host_system_info, host_disk_status, lab_run_status, lab_run_detail, lab_jobs, lab_loop_journal, lab_job_log [tail/grep a job's state/mcp-jobs/<job_id>.log], lab_stage_log [one stage's log in a run report dir], lab_report_grep [grep a run report dir], lab_report_artifacts [list a run report dir's files]) to inspect the LOCAL repo + UTM lab (including the lab inventory, live-lab run results/per-run detail, background jobs + their setup/orchestrator logs, per-stage logs, and the loop findings journal) and answer with cited evidence + an audit trace. Model per call: flash = deepseek-chat (fast), pro = deepseek-reasoner. API key from DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, ~/Desktop/deepseek_api.md, or ~/.deepseek_api_key.
